description: Packed with insightful conversation, Spilling the Tī is a show that introduces and unpacks what sustainability means at Ōtakou Wakaihu Waka - University of Otago. Hosted by Ben Sommerville.

title: AIAIAI — Fernan Rock’s Podcast - My Invisible Alien Friend Series
author: Fernan Rock
description: A space for outsider thinkers, creatives, and anyone who’s ever felt, met, been called, or simply wondered what it means to be an “alien.” Hosted by actor and storyteller Fernan Rock, the podcast explores belonging, identity, and otherness through interviews, humor, and intimate conversations. “Alien” isn’t sci‑fi—it’s a feeling, a label, a mirror. If you’ve ever felt out of place, searching for connection or trying to make sense of how people seem to live their lives so effortlessly in this world, this podcast meets you there.

title: Cynthia Wakeham's Money: A Journey into Mystery and Intrigue
description: When a young lawyer enters the home of a dying woman to draft her will, he unwittingly steps into a web of secrets and intrigue. In his quest to find her legal heirs, he encounters a stunning woman marked by a mysterious scar, whose presence hints at deeper connections and hidden truths. As his search unfolds, unusual events begin to spiral out of control, revealing a world where wealth can be both a blessing and a curse. Anna Katharine Green masterfully blends crime and mystery in this tale, keeping listeners on the edge of their seats with unexpected twists. Discover how ambition and desire intertwine, leading to startling revelations and moral dilemmas. Perfect for fans of classic crime fiction, this gripping story explores the lengths people will go for money and the shadows that loom in their quest for it.

title: Wild & Awake with Bridget Nielsen
author: Bridget Nielsen
description:
Bridget Nielsen shares inspiring and authentic conversations to lift your spirit and support your journey of self-discovery. Wild & Awake delivers empowering resources, tools, and remembrances to reconnection with your sacred nature, become a better human, and expand your consciousness.
Episodes range from transmissions illuminating cosmos consciousness to practical and grounded guidance to navigate your everyday life. Return to your wild, intuitive roots and embody your soul purpose! New episodes go LIVE EVERY TUESDAY about lifestyle, wellness, and spirituality!

title: The Rusty Nail - "Time Capsule 1992"
author: Rob Norwood
description: Time Capsule podcasts are fun because it gives us a chance to look back at the hot topics of that year.  Time Capsule 1992 is no different.  Enjoy!

title: Grind City Hoops Podcast
author: Donovan McLemore
description: Grind City Hoops is your home for smart, passionate basketball conversations. Each week, host Donovan McLemore breaks down the biggest stories from around the basketball world—from the NBA and college basketball to international competitions and the game's biggest stars. Whether it's analyzing blockbuster trades, playoff races, player rivalries, or the future of rising talent, every episode delivers insightful analysis, strong opinions, and engaging discussions for casual fans and die-hard basketball lovers alike. If you love the game beyond the box score, Grind City Hoops is the podcast for you.

title: CLE Radio
author: CLE Radio
description: Welcome to The Land, where we unlock the biggest stories shaping Cleveland and Northeast Ohio. From breaking local news and Browns, Guardians, and Cavaliers updates to concerts, festivals, business headlines, and the people making a difference across The Land, we’ve got it covered.

title: Creepy Colorado With Kai
author: Kai and Matt
description:
Colorado has always been a place of mystery. From the ghost towns left behind by gold rush miners to mountain passes that Indigenous peoples have called sacred for thousands of years, the Centennial State holds more stories than most people ever get to hear.


Creepy Colorado with Kai is a podcast that changes that — one spooky story at a time.


Hosted by 7-year-old Kai and her dad Matt, with Bernese Mountain Dog puppy Spooky riding shotgun, the show takes young listeners on investigations into Colorado's most mysterious legends, haunted locations, and paranormal folklore. Each episode balances real history and local lore with Kai's fearless curiosity — and Matt's very relatable tendency to be just a little bit scared.


Built for kids ages 6 to 12 who are obsessed with all things mysterious, Creepy Colorado with Kai proves that the most interesting stories aren't in textbooks — they're in your own backyard. Or your neighborhood park. Or that old trail nobody talks about after dark.

Stay curious. Stay brave. And stay creepy, Colorado.

title: Hold On, Is that true
author: Dr. Amandeep Kaur, Dr. David Pratt
description: We dig into the research so you don't have to. Each episode breaks down what the science actually says about health, fitness, and longevity — no fads, no guesswork, just data-driven answers to the questions you've always wondered about. If you want to make smarter choices about your body based on evidence instead of hype, this is your show.

description: 3 Brothers talking about trucks and Guns

description:
Queer Spaces is a personal storytelling podcast featuring real stories about meaningful places, historical and contemporary, that inspire and shape the LGBTQIA+ community.  Are you an LGBTQIA+ person with a queer space story to tell?

title: Critical Thought Unfiltered Podcast
author: Critical Thought Unfiltered
description: Rooted in the richness of Black culture and driven by the power of our stories, this podcast is a weekly gathering place for conversation, connection, and celebration. From unpacking current events to highlighting overlooked voices, we dive deep into the experiences that shape our community. Honest talk, uplifting energy, and unapologetically Black perspectives—this is where we honor the past, live in the present, and build the future.

title: The Border Legion: Love and Duty in the Wild West
description: In the rugged landscape near the Idaho border, Jack Kells, a hardened man of the Border Legion, faces a conflict that could change his life forever. Capturing the heart of Miss Joan Randle, a spirited young woman taken prisoner by his own men, Jack grapples with his past and the choices that define him. As he navigates the complexities of love, duty, and loyalty, listeners will discover a gripping story of redemption and the power of the human spirit. Zane Grey masterfully weaves a narrative filled with tension, romance, and the stark realities of frontier life. Through Jack's journey, we explore themes of sacrifice, courage, and the struggle to find one's true self amidst chaos. Join Jack and Joan as they confront their fates, challenging the norms of their time and discovering what it means to truly love and be free.

title: The Metropolis: Portrait of the Wealthy Elite
description: In Upton Sinclair's 'Metropolis', the vibrant yet tumultuous world of New York City's wealthy elite comes to life. Allan Montague, a thirty-year-old lawyer from Mississippi, arrives in the city with his mother and cousin, Alice, eager to reunite with his brother, Oliver. What Allan finds is a dazzling social scene filled with power, ambition, and a whirlwind of high society events. As he navigates this new environment, he uncovers the stark contrasts between wealth and struggle, ambition and morality. This gripping tale explores the lives of the elite and the hidden truths behind their extravagant lifestyles. Sinclair's insightful storytelling delves into themes of class, ambition, and the American Dream, making 'Metropolis' a poignant reflection of society in the early 20th century. Experience the allure and complexities of a world where fortunes are made and lost, and the quest for identity unfolds amidst the backdrop of one of the most iconic cities in the world.

title: Diary of a Resident Doctor
author: Resident Doctor
description:
Every doctor has a diary they never wrote. This is mine.

Diary of a Resident is a podcast told entirely in diary entries, real stories, real emotions, from inside the life of an NHS doctor. The highs that nobody talks about enough, and the lows that nobody talks about at all. No filter, no textbook, no performance. Just the honest account of what it actually feels like to do this job.

title: A Mating in the Wilds: Redemption and Survival
description: In the remote North Woods, Stane, a former convict, has built a solitary life as a trapper. Haunted by his past injustices, he believes his wrongful conviction is tied to Ainley, an old acquaintance from their school days. When Stane visits a trading post, he unexpectedly reunites with Ainley, only to find himself drawn into a web of mystery and danger. The stakes rise when Helen, the niece of a powerful official, mysteriously vanishes, leaving behind questions and fears. As Stane navigates the wild landscape, he encounters two remarkable women: Miskodeed, a striking Indian maiden, and Helen, whose disappearance complicates their lives even further. This gripping adventure explores themes of trust, betrayal, and the quest for redemption. Will Stane confront his past and uncover the truth behind Helen's disappearance? Join him on a thrilling journey through the wilderness, where every choice could mean life or death.

title: GreedyCorp
author: Matthew
description:
GreedyCorp is a podcast about the companies that lied, cheated, and stole their way to the top and the regular people who paid the price.
Every episode, host Matthew digs into a real corporate scandal from the ground up. The backroom deals. The cooked books. The PR spin designed to make you look the other way. From emissions fraud to billion-dollar accounting scams, these are stories about power, control, corruption, and greed... told the way they should be told: with research, with receipts, and with absolutely no respect for the executives who thought they'd get away with it.
If you've ever felt like the system is rigged in favor of the people at the top, you're not wrong. And this is the show that explains exactly how they do it.
New episodes dropping soon. Follow GreedyCorp on Spotify and Twitter. Support the show and get ad-free episodes, Discord access, and input on future episodes at patreon.com.
Greed never sleeps. But neither do we. And we're petty enough to take notes.

title: The Evil Shepherd: A Defense of Morality
description:
When a businessman is found brutally murdered, all evidence points to his partner, Oliver Hilditch. With the gallows looming, Hilditch's fate hangs in the balance until brilliant defense attorney Francis Ledsam intervenes. Ledsam crafts a defense that seems foolproof, but his triumph is short-lived. He soon learns from Hilditch's wife that her husband is guilty of far worse crimes than murder. Caught in a web of deceit, Ledsam faces an agonizing choice: protect his client or uphold justice.

Set against a backdrop of betrayal and ambition, "Evil Shepherd" dives deep into the complexities of right and wrong. Ledsam's struggle becomes a captivating exploration of morality, love, and the high stakes of legal defense. This crime and mystery fiction will keep you on the edge of your seat, challenging your own beliefs about guilt and innocence. Can love truly blind us to the darkest truths? Join Ledsam as he navigates a world where every decision could lead to disaster, and every revelation unveils deeper horrors.

BRITAIN’S MOST BIZARRE BENEFITS SCANDAL

🗓️ Out 20 June on all podcast platforms.
✨ Full cast to be announced!

title: Bible (DRA), Complete 1899 American Edition by Douay-Rheims Version (DRV)
description:
The Douay–Rheims Bible is a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into English made by members of the Catholic seminary English College, Douai, France. It is the foundation on which nearly all English Catholic versions are still based.

It was translated principally by Gregory Martin, an Oxford-trained scholar, working in the circle of English Catholic exiles on the Continent, under the sponsorship of William (later Cardinal) Allen. The New Testament appeared at Rheims in 1582; the Old Testament at Douai in 1609.

The translation, although competent, exhibited a taste for Latinisms that was not uncommon in English writing of the time.
Between 1749 and 1752, English bishop Richard Challoner substantially revised the translation with an aim to improve readability and comprehensibility. It was first published in America in 1790 by Mathew Carey of Philadelphia. Several American editions followed in the 19th and early 20th centuries; prominent among them the Douay-Rheims 1899 American Edition Version. - Summary by Bible Gateway

Proof-listeners: MLeigh and Mozartjr

title: The Quiet Authority
author: Lee Allen Miller
description:
The loudest voice in the room is rarely the one leading it.
Quiet Authority is a leadership conversation for people who carry real responsibility — executives, founders, civic leaders, and anyone whose decisions affect others. Each episode trades noise for clarity: honest talk about how good leaders actually think, decide, and carry weight without needing to dominate the room.
The show explores the steadier, more durable kind of influence — the kind built on judgment, character, and presence rather than volume. No hot takes. No hustle. Just clear thinking on the decisions that matter.
If you lead quietly and lead well, you're in the right place.
Lee Allen Miller is the founder of MSG Resources, where he keeps a small, invitation-only leadership advisory practice.

description:
Medicine, Dollars & Decisions is a podcast about the life and business of medicine.
Clinicians spend years mastering patient care but receive little education on physician compensation, RVUs, contracts, leadership, investing, and the financial decisions that shape their careers.
Hosted by Bruno F. Casanova, MD, MSCE, MA, FACOG, a gynecologic surgeon and Chair of the Women's Health Department at one of New York City's largest multi-specialty physician groups, each episode explores the life and business of medicine in clear, practical language.
Whether you're a medical student, resident, fellow, physician, nurse practitioner, physician assistant, or healthcare leader, you'll gain the knowledge needed to make smarter career and financial decisions.
Because medicine is more than clinical practice. It's also the decisions you make outside the exam room.
Life and Business in Medicine.
New episodes every week.

title: First Gay Summer
author: R. Solo
description:
At thirty years old, Riley walked into his first gay bar.

 

What followed was a summer of firsts.


First crushes. First heartbreak. First mistakes. And more questionable decisions than he'd care to admit. 


Part memoir, part coming-of-age comedy, First Gay Summer follows Riley as he navigates the messy, hilarious, and unexpectedly emotional journey of growing up religious, coming out later than expected, chasing attention, confusing desire with connection, and discovering that the person he'd spent the whole summer looking for was actually himself.

As secrets unfold and tensions rise, the stakes grow higher, and the truth becomes a chilling quest. Will love conquer all, or will the shadows of deception lead to tragedy? Join this gripping tale of romance, rivalry, and suspense that explores the depths of human desire and the lengths one will go to protect what they cherish. With each twist and turn, you'll be drawn into a world where every character has something to hide, and nothing is as it seems. description:
The Beyond the Training Podcast takes you further and farther after a learning or development session.  Many of us have been to the professional development or training at work, school or in some virtual setting.  And, sure, you looked at the slide deck, took the notes and have some screenshots you might look at later.  Still, there's more conversations to be had, more concepts to uncover, and certainly more to learn.
Join the conversation as we discuss perspectives on navigating complex social dynamics and improving interactions - based on decades of training, learning and development experience.  Together, we'll talk about some transformational and pivotal moments from recent work with individuals and groups from around the country, working hard to do what they can to move from focusing on intent, to realizing great impact. 

Beyond the Training is brought to you by the co-founders of Seven Focus, a multi-faceted consulting team that works in partnership with you as you act on your personal, professional and community goals, engaging you to consider the different layers at play - experiences and understandings - so you succeed, long after our team is gone.

Patchwork fixes and DIY attempts only delay the inevitable, often triggering voided insurance claims and astronomical repair bills.

To safeguard your real estate investment and guarantee flawless system efficiency, partnering with an elite plumbing repair folsom specialist is paramount. True structural restoration demands industrial-grade diagnostic tools and certified precision engineering that off-the-shelf equipment simply cannot provide. Affordable Plumbing Solutions sets the benchmark for local service, delivering 24/7 high-end response, upfront architectural pricing, and absolute operational integrity for discerning property owners throughout the Folsom region. title: Back to the Garden
author: Back to the Garden
description:
Somewhere along the way, most of us learned to hide. We built walls to stay safe, put on masks to stay respected, and got so good at managing life that we forgot what it feels like to be truly known. It started in a garden — and it's in a garden that it gets undone.


Back to the Garden is a space for leaders, pastors, and everyone carrying more than they let on — a place to lay down the self-protection and come home to who you were made to be.


Hosted by James and Daniela, every conversation traces the same journey Scripture keeps returning to: from Eden, where hiding began, through the ache of self-protection, and back toward a life lived unashamed, unhurried, and fully seen.


Expect honest conversation about shame and masks, about leading from wholeness instead of performance, and about the slow, freeing work of letting God meet you exactly as you are.


No performance required. Just come as you are — and find your way back to the garden.

Police officers. Firefighters. Paramedics. Dispatchers. Nurses. Veterans.

They respond to emergencies, witness tragedy, carry the weight of critical incidents, and continue showing up when others need them most.

But who is there for them?

title: Dragnet
description:
You're interested in more information about the Dragnet radio show – excellent choice! It was a groundbreaking program that left a significant mark on media. Here's a deeper dive into what made it so impactful:Unprecedented Realism and Procedural DetailDragnet was a pioneer in the "police procedural" genre, focusing on the meticulous, step-by-step nature of police work.1 This was a stark contrast to the often melodramatic private-detective stories prevalent on radio at the time.

  • Based on Actual Cases: Creator Jack Webb worked closely with the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD). Many episodes were based on real case files, giving the show an air of authenticity. They even used the LAPD's actual radio call sign (KMA367) and names of real department officials.
  • "Just the Facts": Sergeant Joe Friday's famous (though slightly misquoted) line, "Just the facts, ma'am," encapsulates the show's no-nonsense approach. The dialogue was clipped, understated, and sparse, influenced by "hard-boiled" crime fiction.
  • Attention to Detail: Webb was a perfectionist when it came to accurate details. He insisted on precise sound effects, which were considered among the best in radio history. The show would chronicle every aspect of police work, from patrols and paperwork to crime scene investigation, lab work, and questioning.
  • Docudrama Format: The show adopted a semi-documentary style, often starting with the iconic narration: "Ladies and gentlemen: the story you are about to hear is true. Only the names have been changed to protect the innocent." This format blurred the lines between fiction and reality, drawing audiences in.

title: Define with Denise
author: Denise
description:
Define with Denise is a podcast about understanding behavior, creating meaningful change, and asking different questions.

Hosted by Denise—a behavior analyst, behavior change coach, and educator—this podcast explores why we do what we do and how we can intentionally create lives, relationships, and families that align with our values.

Drawing from behavior science, psychology, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), and real-life experience, each episode takes one everyday question and explores what's happening beneath the surface. From parenting and relationships to habits, health, resilience, and the patterns we inherit from our families and culture, these conversations are designed to help behavior make sense—and make change feel possible.

Growing up in a hardworking Vietnamese immigrant family taught Denise the value of respect, resilience, sacrifice, and showing up for the people you love. 

Those lessons continue to shape her work today while also inspiring the questions many of us are asking:

What do we carry forward?

What are we ready to redefine?

Whether you're raising children, hoping to become a parent one day, working with kids, supporting families, or simply trying to understand yourself a little better, you'll find thoughtful conversations, practical tools, and a fresh perspective grounded in both science and compassion.

My hope isn't that you leave with all the answers.

It's that you leave asking different questions.

Because different questions lead to different conversations.

And different conversations create the opportunity for change.

Behavior always makes sense—we just have to understand the story it's telling.

È nella mano di chi impasta, nella pazienza di chi aspetta, nel profumo che riempie una cucina prima ancora del primo morso. La farina non è solo un ingrediente, ma memoria, tecnica, trasformazione.
In questo podcast, Peppone Calabrese, con la guida di Antimo Caputo, racconta la farina e tutto quello che può diventare: perché è capace di piegarsi, resistere, accompagnare e nelle mani giuste riesce a diventare qualcosa di straordinario. Una produzione OnePodcast in collaborazione con Caputo.

description:
There Are Only 12 Notes explores leadership, creativity, change, resilience, and teamwork through the lens of music.

Hosted by Paul Nicholson, author of the new book "There Are Only 12 Notes: Leadership Lessons from Music." There Are Only 12 Notes, the podcast is built around a simple idea: the most complex performances, organizations, and careers are often built from a surprisingly small set of fundamentals. In music, every song begins with the same twelve notes. In leadership, the same principle applies. Clarity, rhythm, trust, listening, adaptation, and purpose are often what separate noise from progress.

Each episode uses stories, reflections, and practical ideas to examine how leaders can simplify complexity, build stronger teams, navigate change, and create work that lasts. Drawing from music, creative production, business, and real-world leadership experience, the show is designed for people who lead teams, manage pressure, guide creative work, or are trying to find a better way to move people forward in business.

This is not a podcast about becoming a musician. It is a podcast about becoming a more thoughtful, steady, and effective leader by paying closer attention to what music has been teaching us all along.

No perfection, no judgment just two people on the journey together, sharing what actually works. Ready to glow from the inside out? Hit play and let's grow together.

author: QCTV
description: Podcast versions of your favorite QCTV shows.

title: Paradise Projects
author: H. Sanches
description: Electronic Music from Ibiza

title: The BGC Podcast
author: Vick and Laura
description:
Welcome to The BGC Podcast (Big Gay Chat), where Vick and Laura, two vibrant Gay women residing on the sun-kissed shores of the South Coast of England, invite you into their world of unfiltered conversations, boundless laughter, and heartfelt discussions.

Dive into the rainbow spectrum of LGBTQIA+ themes with us as we explore love, life, and everything in between, all while sharing our own experiences and perspectives. But hey, we're not just about LGBTQIA+ topics – expect a smorgasbord of discussions ranging from pop culture and current events to personal anecdotes and beyond.

What sets us apart? Well, aside from our infectious laughter and genuine camaraderie, we have a little segment that's become a fan favorite: "Vick's Icks"! Tune in each week as Vick shares her quirky, amusing, and sometimes downright bizarre anecdotes from her daily adventures.

So, whether you're part of the LGBTQIA+ community or an ally looking to broaden your horizons, join us for a dose of fun, insight, and plenty of Big Gay Chat vibes. Get ready to laugh, learn, and maybe even shed a tear or two along the way. 

Welcome to the BGC family!

title: Soothing Stanzas - Gentle Verses for Quiet Nights
author: Florentino Ariza
description: Each episode of Soothing Stanzas offers a single poem — from a lovestruck scribe in ancient Egypt to Pablo Neruda, Tagore. Poets from the ages and here and now — savored slowly, with warmth and full attention. Together we'll sit with its wisdom, its joy, its ache. Because poetry holds all of it: love and loss, solitude and seasons, the small mysteries of being alive, the grandeur of this vast and wonderful cosmos. And somehow, it makes all of it better. New episodes every week. Best taken with some camomile tea, and a closed door.

title: The Gold Bag: A Millionaire's Murder
description: When a millionaire is found murdered in his study, the scene is set for a captivating whodunit. Suspicion hangs in the air as the household staff, including mysterious servants and a beguiling niece, becomes embroiled in the investigation. Enter a sharp-witted detective, reminiscent of Sherlock Holmes, who must untangle a web of deceit and hidden motives. With a tangled will at stake and secrets lurking in every corner, this story takes listeners on a thrilling journey filled with twists and turns. As the detective delves deeper, each clue reveals more than just the identity of the killer; it uncovers the true nature of wealth and trust. Join the investigation and see if you can solve the puzzle before the final reveal. Perfect for fans of classic crime fiction, this tale offers a blend of suspense, intrigue, and timeless storytelling that will keep you guessing until the very end.

title: Regional Equips
author: David Smith
description: brings together biblical teaching from Four12 Regional Equip gatherings around the world to strengthen believers, equip leaders, and build healthy, fruitful local churches.  Each episode is rooted in Scripture and speaks to the realities of following Jesus today. Whether you need encouragement, correction, fresh vision, or practical wisdom, these messages will help you grow in faith, stand firm in the gospel, and live wholeheartedly for Christ.  Join us as we pursue Jesus, are equipped for every good work, and see His Church strengthened to make an enduring impact for His glory.

title: The Pop Bible Study
author: Patrick Scott
description: Welcome to The Pop Bible Study. Every great pop album has a story waiting to be uncovered. Together, we'll explore the lyrics, themes, production, history, and cultural impact behind pop's most influential records. Created as the companion podcast to The Pop Confessional, this is where the reaction ends, and the study begins.

title: Steep Turnpike Evangelical Church
author: Steep Turnpike Evangelical Church
description: Weekly talks and sermons from the pulpit of Steep Turnpike Evangelical Church, Matlock.

title: The Daily Oink
author: Soul & Jeaux & Sway
description: We're tired of bad news. We bet you are too. The Daily Oink is a weekly comedy podcast where two hosts — two Americans & one Spanish — trade the most absurd news stories from their corners of the world. He read American weirdness to Them. They read global chaos to him. Neither of us sees it coming. Together we laugh, we react, and we remind ourselves that no matter where you live, humans are out here doing the most ridiculous stuff imaginable. Funny news, weekly. No politics. No doom. Just a flying pig and a lot of "wait, WHAT?"

title: The Connecticut Caselaw Podcast™
author: James Ruane
description:
This podcast covers weekly released decisions from the Connecticut Supreme and Appellate courts as well as revisits seminal cases throughout the years. The podcast breaks down the recent decisions to give you an up to the minute understanding of the current state of the law in the areas of Criminal Law, Injury Law and Family Law.

Attorney Jay Ruane, Juris 415988 is responsible for the content of this advertisement.

title: BGD77
description: এছাড়াও BGD77 -এ নিয়মিতভাবে আকর্ষণীয় বিভিন্ন প্রোমোশনাল প্রোগ্রাম চালু করা হয়, যা বিশ্বস্ত সদস্যদের প্রতি কৃতজ্ঞতার নিদর্শন হিসেবে প্রদান করা হয়। প্রতিটি ইভেন্ট সৃজনশীলভাবে পরিকল্পিত, যা ব্যবহারকারীদের মধ্যে নতুন কিছু আবিষ্কারের উত্তেজনা ও আগ্রহ তৈরি করে। প্রতিটি কার্যক্রমের মাধ্যমে প্রাণবন্ত পরিবেশ ছড়িয়ে পড়ে, যা একটি ইতিবাচক ও উদ্দীপনাময় পারস্পরিক যোগাযোগের ক্ষেত্র তৈরি করে। এই সব উপাদানই প্ল্যাটফর্মটিকে দীর্ঘমেয়াদে ডিজিটাল বিনোদন বাজারে তার আকর্ষণ ধরে রাখতে সহায়তা করেছে। WEB: https://bgd77mobi.com/

title: High Wind in Jamaica - A Tragicomic Pirate Adventure
description: In the heart of the Caribbean, a group of children finds themselves caught in a whirlwind of adventure and mischief. Richard Hughes’ 'High Wind in Jamaica' invites listeners into a world where childhood innocence meets the harsh realities of piracy. This isn’t your typical pirate story; the characters are layered, flawed, and uniquely human. The pirates, with their distinct personalities and complex relationships, navigate life aboard a schooner while reluctantly caring for the children. As these young adventurers engage with their captors, unexpected bonds form amid the chaos and danger. In this tragi-comic narrative, the clash of innocence and adulthood offers insights into human nature and the unpredictability of life at sea. Experience the thrill of adventure and the depth of emotional connection as the story unfolds, revealing how perceptions can differ between the innocent and the experienced. This captivating tale challenges the norms of both childhood and piracy, leaving listeners pondering the true meaning of freedom and friendship. Join the crew on this unforgettable journey where laughter and peril go hand in hand.

title: The Structure Drives Results Pod
author: EvanHillTrains
description:
Evan Hill and the occasional special guest talk about all things fitness.
I hope you enjoy the podcast and learn something new from each episode. 
If you like this podcast, please give it a review to help support the channel. 
Make sure to follow me on Instagram for more!

title: BUILT THROUGH IT
author: CLINTON IYEN
description:
Built Through It is a podcast that uncovers inspiring stories of resilience from around the world. Hosted by Clinton Iyen, each episode explores the struggles, setbacks, and breakthroughs behind remarkable journeys. Through honest conversations, listeners discover hope, courage, and the strength to keep moving forward. Because behind every success is a story of perseverance—and resilience is something we're built through.
Real Stories of Resilience From Around the World.

title: Mind Over Habit
author: Moses King
description: Mind Over Habit Podcast is where real conversations spark real change. Hosted by Moses, this show explores habits, addiction, healing, mental wellness, personal growth, and the stories that shape who we are. Through honest discussions with inspiring guests from around the world, you'll gain practical insights, fresh perspectives, and motivation to break unhealthy patterns and build a better life. If you love meaningful conversations, powerful stories, and personal transformation, this podcast is for you. Change begins in the mind. Join the journey.

title: Boarding School Matters
author: Sarah Capewell and Melissa Hatton
description:
Choosing a boarding school is one of the biggest decisions a family can make. But how do you get beyond the glossy prospectus and discover what boarding life is really like?

Boarding School Matters is the podcast that gets underneath the brochure, bringing you honest conversations, expert insight and practical advice about UK boarding schools.


Hosted by Sarah Capewell, a boarding school consultant, former Head of Boarding and parent of two boarders, and Melissa Hatton, former Head of Admissions and Marketing, boarding school parent and former boarder herself, the podcast explores every aspect of boarding life from the perspectives of parents, pupils and the professionals who know it best.


Whether you're considering boarding for the first time, comparing schools, navigating admissions, or supporting an international family through the process, each episode provides clear, impartial guidance to help you make informed decisions with confidence.


Expect conversations about:


• Choosing the right boarding school
• Full, weekly and flexi boarding
• Admissions, scholarships and bursaries
• Boarding houses and pastoral care
• Student wellbeing and school culture
• International boarding
• Life as a boarder, parent and house parent
• Interviews with heads, admissions teams, pupils and education experts


If you're looking for honest advice about UK boarding schools from people who've lived and worked in the sector, you're in the right place.

description: Since 2010, The China History Podcast, presented by Laszlo Montgomery, has brought you hundreds of episodes of curated historical topics from China's antiquity to modern times.

Việc cập nhật giao diện, nâng cấp tính năng và cải thiện hiệu năng hoạt động là những yếu tố quan trọng giúp duy trì sức cạnh tranh. title: Digital Access Academy
description: Digital Access Academy is a technology education podcast that makes online platforms easier to understand. We share clear, practical guidance on employee portals, student systems, workplace tools, educational technology, account security, login troubleshooting, and digital best practices. Whether you're accessing a work portal, navigating a school platform, or looking for simple solutions to common online issues, each episode provides straightforward information to help you use digital services with confidence, safely and efficiently.

title: C Note: Chase the Music
author: MOTR Podcast Network
description:
In the late 1990s, C Note was one of the fastest-rising boy bands in America. They toured, built a devoted fanbase, and stood on the edge of the kind of success most aspiring musicians spend their lives chasing.
Then life happened...
In C Note: Chase the Music , nephew and uncle Remso W. Martinez and Brody Martinez revisit the unlikely journey that took Brody from the heights of pop stardom to the decades that followed. Across five episodes, they explore ambition, family, sacrifice, missed opportunities, reinvention, and what happens when the spotlight fades but the music never truly leaves.
Part oral history, part family conversation, and part reflection on the pursuit of dreams, C Note: Chase the Music is a story about more than a boy band. It's about the choices that shape our lives, the roads we never expected to travel, and the things we're still chasing long after the applause ends.
Produced by MOTR Podcast Network.

title: Juliverse
author: Julsie
description:
Welcome to the Juliverse. Where every bad decision eventually becomes an episode.
I’m Julie, and curiosity usually gets the best of me.
This isn’t a self-help podcast and it’s not an interview show. One week I might be talking about chasing my bucket-list dreams and side quests. The next, I’m dissecting random thoughts I’ve apparently found meaning in.
So come for the stories and stay because you already accidentally pressed play.

title: EntrepreNourie
author: Phil Nourie | Reignite Your Inner Child
description:
EntrepreNourie is a 25-minute podcast that explores what happens when we reconnect with our inner child. Designed for accomplished people "not done yet", and hosted by four-time entrepreneur, Phil Nourie, each episode features conversations with real people like you, exploring how they navigate change and discover meaning.
Music credits: The Saturdays, a Northwest Philly-based indie/folk trio featuring bassist David Arms, percussionist Buck Buchanan, and lead singer, guitarist, and keyboardist, Rich Nourie. Check out their latest album, "One Afternoon in April".

title: Trying To Figure This Out
author: Morris
description:
Honest conversations for the chapters of life nobody prepared us for.

There comes a point in life when the questions begin to change.

You stop wondering what's next on the checklist and start wondering what's next for you.

Trying To Figure This Out is a podcast about navigating life's transitions with curiosity, honesty, and a little humor. Through personal stories and meaningful conversations, we explore purpose, change, relationships, work, family, and the questions that often don't have easy answers.

This isn't a podcast about having life figured out.

It's about becoming brave enough to ask better questions.

Because sometimes the most important conversations aren't about finding the answers...

They're about discovering you're not asking those questions alone.

title: Between Meetings: Refill and Reset with Dr. Maritza
author: Maritza Salazar Campo
description:
Between Meetings offers real conversations shaping
today's workplaces. It cuts through the noise with forward-thinking
insights, bold questions, and powerful strategies to drive change.
Each episode opens with a bold 3–5 minute pulse check on a pressing
workplace challenge or cultural shift. Then the real talk begins! Maritza
teams up with expert guests and thought leaders to unpack what's going on
on beneath the surface of organizational life – team dynamics, cultural
trends and leadership.
Whether you're making sense of the modern workplace, leading a team,
or managing change, you'll leave each episode with fresh insights and
practical tools you can actually use. Think of it as your on-the-go guide for
meaningful impact, between meetings.
If you're ready to rethink how work works, Between Meetings with Dr.
Maritza is your chance to refill and reset your day!

title: Changing the Conversation About Epilepsy
author: Empowering Epilepsy
description: Changing the Conversation About Epilepsy is a podcast by Empowering Epilepsy focused on educating, empowering, and supporting people impacted by epilepsy and seizure disorders. Featuring epileptologists, specialists, advocates, caregivers, and individuals living with epilepsy, we discuss seizure management, sleep, exercise, mental health, doctor visits, neuropsychology, art therapy, and practical ways to take charge of life with epilepsy.

title: The Andre Price Show
author: BackUpSquad
description: The Andre Price Show is a weekly podcast hosted by Andre Price that focuses on local stories and industry spotlights spanning animation, politics, and entrepreneurship.

description: The Holling Effect is a podcast hosted by Colton Holling, a college student in Los Angeles with a passion for the performing arts, film, and the world around him. Every episode is different. Sometimes there's a guest, sometimes it's just Colton. Sometimes it's deep, sometimes it's just fun. No formula, no filter. Just unique people having unique conversations. Welcome to The Holling Effect.

title: Beyond Borders
author: AP Globale
description:
Beyond Borders is a premium podcast series from APG Originals featuring diplomats, policymakers, business leaders, economists, strategic thinkers, and global affairs experts.

Through insightful and in-depth conversations, the series explores geopolitics, global trade, economic transformation, technology, diplomacy, leadership, and the forces shaping the future world order.

From BRICS and emerging markets to international cooperation, innovation, and strategic affairs, Beyond Borders brings together influential voices to discuss the ideas, challenges, and opportunities that transcend national boundaries.

Because some conversations shape nations, economies, and the future itself.

title: No One's Going To Find This
author: youngmichebaby
description: No One's Going to Find This is a podcast about the conversations that don't fit neatly into a headline. Each episode explores what's on my mind from pop culture and reality TV to politics, social media, and the way we think. Rather than telling you what to believe, I ask questions, challenge assumptions, and examine where culture, psychology, and current events intersect. Some episodes are serious. Some are lighthearted. All of them are driven by curiosity. Whether we're unpacking a viral moment, a political talking point, or a cultural trend, the goal is the same: to think a little deeper

title: School Cop Channel Podcast
author: Captain OhGee
description: School and Law-Related issues for Parents, Families, and Educators.  Information presented by a School Resource Officer to Listners.

title: Sambandh
author: The Royal Norwegian Embassy in New Delhi
description:
Are you curious about the relationship between the world’s largest democracy and Norway? How do they cooperate in research, diplomacy, and trade? What are the biggest differences?  Or do you want to do business in the world’s fastest-growing economy?  
The Royal Norwegian Embassy in New Delhi invites interesting guests to explore the stories and partnerships shaping the relationship between Norway and India.

title: Healing Lyme with Lauren
author: Lauren Stoll
description:
Chronic illness is a cruel and lonely journey. Lauren knows this firsthand in her own battle with Lyme disease, which she was first diagnosed with when she was nine years old. Healing Lyme With Lauren is a safe space for silent sufferers to feel heard & encouraged, truth to be told, and for Lyme warriors to feel less alone.
*This is not medical advice. Consult your doctor.
Lauren is an author, Lyme coach, & digital designer. For Lauren’s favorite healing tools & other resources, visit: 
www.healinglymewithlauren.com or
https://campsite.bio/healingwithlo
 
Instagram: @healinglymewithlo

title: Zay’s Lounge
author: Xzavaion Zay Digsby
description:
Welcome to Zay's Lounge - a real space for real conversations.
This is where we talk about the grind, the journey to success, mental health, and the raw truth behind what it takes to become who you're meant to be. I speak from experience, pain, growth, and purpose.
This isn't just a podcast-it's a safe space, a movement, and a family. Here, you can speak your truth without being judged. We don't fake it here. We feel it, we heal through it, and we build from it. If you've ever felt misunderstood, overlooked, or counted out-this lounge was made for you. 
Email- zaylounge26@gmail.com

title: (@Puali00)
author: CHERRY, PARKER
description:
Documentation of the daily grind from Dallas to NYC—balancing elite hospitality, [Design-Service] operations, and soNeu clothing.
Running the frameworks that make me #elite.

title: Becoming: An Evidence-Based Guide To Being Human
author: Jennifer Ewing
description: Life changes all of us. None of us escapes suffering, but all of us can learn from it. Becoming is an evidence-based psychology podcast exploring what it means to be human through psychology, neuroscience, and behavioral science. Each lesson asks one important question, follows the research wherever it leads, and translates complex science into everyday language. This isn't self-help. It's a curriculum for understanding ourselves, each other, and the lifelong process of becoming.

title: Altadena Wildlife Chronicles & Property Security
author: All Star Animal Trapping
description:
Comprehensive coyote preventive services altadena ca stand as the primary operational baseline required to safeguard modern foothill residential lines from encroaching apex wildlife packs. When native predators drift into neighborhood borders, property lines face persistent security issues and direct safety hazards. This professional-grade series breaks down technical property monitoring, structural exclusion setups, and strategic canine behavior tracking protocols.

Relying on temporary deterrents or unverified wildlife control methods leaves assets exposed to long-term degradation. Securing local property boundaries demands precision-engineered barriers and humane wildlife management strategies that permanently redirect animal routes away from local living spaces. All Star Animal Trapping delivers the industry premium standard, providing upfront fixed rates, certified exclusion layouts, and permanent environmental protection for your landscape. Stream our structural safety archives to secure your perimeter today.

title: Greenwood Hills Bible Conferences & Camps
author: Greenwood Hills
description:
Greenwood Hills desires for Christians to grow closer to the Lord through His Word. Located in Fayetteville PA, Greenwood Hills hosts many types of Conferences, Camps, and Retreats for all ages. Generations have come to Greenwood Hills from across America and around the world, as gifted Bible teachers have opened the Scriptures. We hope that these messages help to explore God’s word and grow in His beloved Son, Jesus Christ.
For more information please visit www.greenwoodhills.net.

description: Vbbn

description: Call Me Mister is a podcast featuring honest conversations with Christian men in education. Through stories of career, calling, family, leadership, and faith, we explore what it really looks like to build a meaningful and sustainable life while serving the next generation.

title: Jerusalem Summit
author: ICEJ
description: Welcome to the Jerusalem Summit channel, a central space for updates, session resources, and key discussions as we equip leaders, strengthen the Church’s witness, and stand for biblical truth regarding Israel.

title: Dr. Bipul Singh
author: Dr. Bipul Singh
description: Principal Dr. Bipul Singh is a highly accomplished and dedicated educational leader passionate about nurturing young minds. Since June 2019, Dr. Bipul Singh has served as the Principal of Oglethorpe Ave. Elementary School in Athens, Georgia. With an extensive 22 years of experience in education, including roles as a special education teacher, assistant principal, and principal, Dr. Singh brings a wealth of expertise and a proven track record of success.

description:
Authentic Islamic reminders rooted in the Qur’an and Sunnah, encouraging Muslims to strengthen their tawheed, purify their hearts, and live according to Islam. By Akhi Amir

title: AU88Biz
description: Chào mừng bạn đến với podcast AU88Biz, nơi chia sẻ những thông tin mới nhất về mã khuyến mãi, ưu đãi trực tuyến và các mẹo giúp tiết kiệm chi phí khi sử dụng dịch vụ đặt đồ ăn. Chúng tôi cập nhật nội dung thường xuyên để giúp người nghe dễ dàng tìm hiểu cách sử dụng mã khuyến mãi, theo dõi các chương trình ưu đãi mới và khám phá những phương pháp tiết kiệm hiệu quả. Mỗi tập podcast đều mang đến thông tin rõ ràng, dễ hiểu và hữu ích dành cho những ai muốn cập nhật các chương trình ưu đãi mới nhất từ AU88Biz.

title: The High Performance Hive Podcast
author: Harmony Jade
description:
Do you want to know how to stop the burnout cycles, find a life and business that feels aligned AF and get after your BIG AF dreams? 
Then it's time to listen up to the people who are out there showing you that it IS possible to live your best life, make money and feel lit up on life without burning the candle at both ends.
Welcome to The High Performance Hive Podcast! author: TOKYO FM
description:
本当は丸くおさめたいのに、どうしてもおさめられない日々のモヤモヤや割り切れない感情。

この番組では、そんな人間ならではの“丸くおさまらない話”を持ち寄り、リスナーの皆さんとシェアしながら、一緒に少しだけ成長していくことを目指します。


右か左か。推しかアンチか。何でもハッキリさせ過ぎたり。
周りの空気を読んでなんとなく合わせてみたり。
無理してはみ出す必要はないけれど、無理に丸くおさめなくたっていいんじゃない？


正論のアドバイスや論破は一切なし。 「これって自分だけ？」「なんでこうなった？」という日々の些細な引っかかりに、答えは出ないかもしれないけれど、等身大の岸谷蘭丸がリスナーと共感し寄り添います。

title: Transcendent Embrace: A metaphysical memoir

description:
Transcendent Embrace: A Metaphysical Memoir is an intimate exploration of the moments when deeper dimensions of consciousness break through the boundaries of everyday life, carrying us into states of profound love and expanded awareness. Drawing from a lifetime of extraordinary experiences, the author invites us into a luminous frontier where the personal self meets the transcendent soul.


Part One begins in adolescence, when Dianne endeavored to master out-of-body travel, also called “astral projection.” Her first effort almost ended tragically. But soon she learned how to communicate with loved ones and transverse geographic distances while her physical body slept.  Through astral eyes, she beheld all of creation as radiantly alive, aware, and ever enfolding us in its eternity. These revelations opened vast new realities, inspiring a lifelong quest to explore wonders of the soul.

Whether you're entering the Nether for the first time or preparing for the Ender Dragon, this episode is packed with practical advice, useful tricks, and beginner-friendly strategies to help you survive and make it home with your loot. title: Bible (ASV) 39: Malachi by American Standard Version
description: Malachi (or Malachias, מַלְאָכִי, Malʾaḫi, Mál'akhî) is a book of the Bible Old Testament and Jewish Tanakh, written by the prophet Malachi. Possibly this is not the name of the author, since Malachi means 'my messenger' or 'my angel' in Hebrew. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 38: Zechariah by American Standard Version
description: Zechariah’s ministry took place during the reign of Darius the Great (Zechariah 1:1), and was contemporary with Haggai in a post-exilic world after the fall of Jerusalem in 586/7 BC. Ezekiel and Jeremiah wrote prior to the fall of Jerusalem, while continuing to prophesy in the earlier exile period. Scholars believe Ezekiel, with his blending of ceremony and vision, heavily influenced the visionary works of Zechariah 1-8.Zechariah is specific about dating his writing (520-518 BC). During the Exile many Jews were taken to Babylon, where the prophets told them to make their homes (Jeremiah 29), suggesting they would spend a long period of time there. Eventually freedom did come to many Israelites, when Cyrus the Great overtook the Babylonians in 539 BC. In 538 BC, the famous Edict of Cyrus was released, and the first return took place under Shebazzar. After the death of Cyrus in 530 BC, Darius consolidated power and took office in 522 BC. His system divided the different colonies of the empire into easily manageable districts overseen by governors. Zerubbabel comes into the story, appointed by Darius as governor over the district of Yehud (Judah). Under the reign of Darius Zechariah also emerged, centering around the rebuilding of the temple. Unlike the Babylonians, the Persian Empire went to great lengths to keep “cordial relations” between vassal and lord. The rebuilding of the temple was encouraged by the leaders of the empire in hopes that it would strengthen the authorities in local contexts. This policy was good politics on the part of the Persians, and the Jews viewed it as a blessing by Yahweh. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 37: Haggai by American Standard Version
description: The Book of Haggai is a book of the Tanakh (Hebrew Bible) and of the Old Testament, written by the prophet Haggai. It was written in 520 BC some 18 years after Cyrus had conquered Babylon and issued a decree in 538 BC allowing the captive Jews to return to Judea. He saw the restoration of the temple as necessary for the restoration of the religious practices and a sense of peoplehood after a long exile. It consists of two brief, comprehensive chapters. The object of the prophet is generally urging the people to proceed with the rebuilding of the second Jerusalem temple in 521 BC after the return of the deportees. Haggai attributes a recent drought to the peoples' refusal to rebuild the temple, which he sees as key to Jerusalem’s glory. The book ends with the prediction of the downfall of kingdoms, with one Zerubbabel, governor of Judah, as the Lord’s chosen leader. The language here is not as finely wrought as in some other books of the minor prophets, yet the intent seems straightforward. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 36: Zephaniah by American Standard Version
description: The superscription of the Book of Zephaniah attributes its authorship to “Zephaniah son of Cushi son of Gedaliah son of Amariah son of Hezekiah, in the days of King Josiah son of Amon of Judah” (1:1, NRSV). All that is known of Zephaniah comes from the text. The superscription of the book is lengthier than most and contains two features. The name Cushi, Zephaniah’s father, means ‘Ethiopian’. In a society where genealogy was considered extremely important because of God's covenant with Abraham and his descendants, the author may have felt compelled to establish his Hebrew lineage. In fact, this lineage is traced back to Hezekiah, who was king of Judah. The author of Zephaniah does not shrink from condemning the Cushites or Ethiopians. Chapter 2:12 contains a succinct but unequivocal message: “You also, O Ethiopians, / Shall be killed by my sword.” Zephaniah’s familial connection with King Hezekiah may have also legitimized his harsh indictment of the royal city in 3:1-7. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 35: Habakkuk by American Standard Version
description:
Practically nothing is known about Habakkuk's personal history, except for what can be inferred from the text of his book, which consists of five oracles about the Chaldeans (Babylonians) and a song of praise to God. Since the Chaldean rise to power is dated c. 612 BC, it is assumed he was active about that time, making him an early contemporary of Jeremiah and Zephaniah. Jewish sources, however, do not group him with those two prophets, who are often placed together, so it is possible that he was slightly earlier than they. Because the final chapter of his book is a song, it is sometimes assumed in Jewish tradition that he was a member of the tribe of Levi, which served as musicians in Solomon's Temple. According to the Zohar (Volume 1, page 8b) Habakkuk is the boy born to the Shunamite woman through Elisha's blessing. Habakkuk is unique among the prophets in that he openly questions the wisdom of God.[citation needed] In the first part of the first chapter, the Prophet sees the injustice among his people and asks why God does not take action: "1:2 Yahweh, how long will I cry, and you will not hear? I cry out to you 'Violence!' and will you not save?" - (World English Bible).
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 34: Nahum by American Standard Version
description:
Nahum prophesied, according to some, in the beginning of the reign of Ahaz (740s BC). Others, however, think that his prophecies are to be referred to the latter half of the reign of Hezekiah (700s BC). Probably the book was written in Jerusalem, where he witnessed the invasion of Sennacherib and the destruction of his host (2 Kings 19:35). And still others support the idea that the "book of vision" was written shortly before the fall of Nineveh (612 BCE). This theory is evidenced by the fact that the oracles must be dated after the Assyrian destruction of Thebes in 663 BCE as this event is mentioned in Nah 3:8.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 33: Micah by American Standard Version
description:
The book may be divided into three sections:
Chapters 1–3 mainly consist of oracles of judgment.
Chapters 4–5 of oracles of hope.
Chapters 6–7 begins with judgment and moves to hope.
Chapters 1–3 mainly consist of oracles of judgment. The judgment motif is so strong in this book that Micah only preached about judgment. Judgment in Micah is seen in the destruction of Samaria, in the coming of an invader against Jerusalem, in the greedy land-grabbers' loss of their land and in their being abandoned by Yahweh, in shame for the false prophets, in the siege of Jerusalem and the cleaning of the land from idolatry and militarism.
Chapters 4–5 consist of oracles of hope. The prophet said that those conditions would not prevail forever. Judgment would come but a saved, chastened, and faithful remnant would survive. A new king from the line of David would be replace the present weak king on the throne. He would reign in the majesty of the name of Yahweh. His people would dwell securely and he would be great to the ends of earths.
Chapters 6–7 begin with judgment and move to hope. Micah puts a protest on the people's lips, offering any religious response God cared to ask for. God's indictment becomes specific in 6:9–16. Violence, deception, and crooked business practices were rampant. They would bring desolation and destruction to the land. The reference to Omri and Ahab indicates that the same kinds of corruption that destroyed the northern kingdom had now spread to Judah.
In conclusion, Micah's later hearers take his messages to heart. His words of hope gave them new heart to live as God's people in a darkened world.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 32: Jonah by American Standard Version
description:
In the Hebrew Bible, the Book of Jonah is the fifth book in a series of books called the Minor Prophets. Unlike other prophetic books however, this book is not a record of a prophet’s words toward Israel. Instead of the poetry and prophetic prose of Isaiah or Lamentations, this book tells the story of a reluctant prophet who arguably becomes one of the most effective prophets in the entire Bible.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 31: Obadiah by American Standard Version
description: "The Book of Obadiah is found in both the Hebrew Bible and the Old Testament of the Christian Bible, where it is the shortest book, only one chapter long. Its authorship is generally attributed to a person named Obadiah, which means “servant (or worshipper) of the Lord”. Obadiah is classified as a "minor prophet" in the Christian Bible due to the brevity of the writing (only 21 verses) and the content (prophetic material). An Old Testament prophet was (professedly) not only a person who was given divine insight into future events, but a person whom the Lord used to declare his word. The first nine verses in the book foretell total destruction in the land of Edom at the hand of the Lord. Obadiah writes that this destruction will be so complete that it will be even worse than a thief who comes at night, for not even a thief would destroy everything. The Lord will allow all allies of Edom to turn away and help chase Edom out of its land. What is the reason for such a harsh punishment? Verses ten through fourteen explain that when Israel (the Lord’s chosen people) was attacked, Edom refused to help them, thus acting like an enemy. What is even worse is that Edom and Israel share a common blood line through their founders who were brothers, Jacob and Esau. Because of this gross neglect of a relative, Edom will be covered with shame and destroyed forever. The final verses, fifteen through twenty-one, depict the restoration of Israel and the wiping out of the Edomites. Verse eighteen says that there will be no survivors from the house of Esau once the destruction is complete. Israel will become a holy place and its people will return from exile and inhabit the land once inhabited by the Edomites. The final verse of the prophecy places the Lord as King who will rule over all the mountains of Edom." (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 30: Amos by American Standard Version
description: "The Book of Amos is one of the books of the Nevi'im (Hebrew: "prophets") and of the Christian Old Testament. Amos is one of the minor prophets. Amos was the first biblical prophet whose words were recorded in a book, an older contemporary of Hosea and Isaiah. He was active c 750 BC during the reign of Jeroboam II. He lived and prophesied in the southern kingdom of Judah. His major themes of social justice, God's omnipotence, and divine judgment became staples of prophecy." (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 27: Daniel by American Standard Version
description:
The Book of Daniel (דניאל), originally written in Hebrew and Aramaic, is a book in both the Hebrew Bible (Tanakh) and the Christian Old Testament. The book is set during the Babylonian Captivity, a period when Jews were deported and exiled to Babylon following the Siege of Jerusalem of 597 BC. The book revolves around the figure of Daniel, an Israelite who becomes an adviser to Nebuchadnezzar, the ruler of Babylon from 605 BC - 562 BC. The book has two distinct parts: a series of six narratives (chapters one to six) and four apocalyptic visions (chapters seven to twelve). The narratives take the form of court stories which focus on tests of religious fidelity involving Daniel and his friends (chapters one, three and six), and Daniel's interpretation of royal dreams and visions (chapters two, four and five). In the second part of the book, Daniel recounts his reception of dreams, visions and angelic interpretations in the first person.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 29: Joel by American Standard Version
description: "The Book of Joel is part of the Jewish Tanakh, and also the Old Testament of the Christian Bible. Joel is part of a group of twelve prophetic books known as the Minor Prophets or simply as The Twelve; the distinction 'minor' indicates the short length of the text in relation to the larger prophetic texts by the "Major Prophets" known as the Nevi'im." (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 28: Hosea by American Standard Version
description: Hosea was the son of Beeri and a prophet in Israel in the 8th century BCE. He is one of the Twelve Prophets of the Jewish Hebrew Bible, also known as the Minor Prophets of the Christian Old Testament. We know practically nothing about the life or social status of Hosea. According to the Book of Hosea, he married the prostitute Gomer, the daughter of Diblatayim, at God's command. He lived in the Northern Kingdom in the period 740–725 BCE. In Hosea 5:8 ff., there is a reference to the wars which led to the capture of the kingdom by the Assyrians (ca. 734–732 BCE). It is not certain if he has also experienced the destruction of Samaria, which is foreseen in Hosea 14:1. Hosea's family life reflected the "adulterous" relationship which Israel had built with polytheistic gods. His children's names made them like walking prophecies of the fall of the ruling dynasty and the severed covenant with God — much like the prophet Isaiah a generation later. Hosea is often seen as a "prophet of doom", but underneath his message of destruction is a promise of restoration." (From Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 25: Lamentations by American Standard Version
description:
The Book of Lamentations (Hebrew: אֵיכָה‎, Eikha, ʾēḫā(h)) is a book of the Bible Old Testament and Jewish Tanakh. It is traditionally read by the Jewish people on Tisha B'Av, the fast day that commemorates the destruction of the Temple in Jerusalem. It is called in the Hebrew canon 'Eikhah, meaning "How," being the formula for the commencement of a song of wailing. It is the first word of the book (see 2 Sam. 1:19-27). The Septuagint adopted the name rendered "Lamentations" (Greek threnoi = Hebrew qinoth) now in common use, to denote the character of the book, in which the prophet mourns over the desolations brought on Jerusalem and the Holy Land by the Chaldeans. In the Hebrew Bible it is placed among the Ketuvim, the Writings.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 23: Isaiah by American Standard Version
description:
"The Book of Isaiah (Hebrew: ספר ישעיה‎) is a book of the Bible traditionally attributed to the Prophet Isaiah, who lived in the second half of the 8th century BC. In the first 39 chapters, Isaiah prophesies doom for a sinful Judah and for all the nations of the world that oppose God. The last 27 chapters prophesy the restoration of the nation of Israel. This section includes the Songs of the Suffering Servant, four separate passages that Christians believe prefigure the coming of Jesus Christ, and which are otherwise traditionally thought to refer to the nation of Israel. This second of the book's two major sections also includes prophecies of a new creation in God's glorious future kingdom."
(Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 22: Song of Solomon (version 2) by American Standard Version
description:
The Song of Songs (Hebrew title שיר השירים, Shir ha-Shirim), is a book of the Hebrew Bible—Tanakh or Old Testament—one of the five megillot (scrolls). It is also known as the Song of Solomon or as Canticles, the latter from the shortened and anglicized Vulgate title Canticum Canticorum, "Song of Songs" in Latin. It is known as Aisma in the Septuagint, which is short for Αισμα ᾀσμτων, Aisma aismatôn, "Song of Songs" in Greek.
(From Wikipedia)

title: Bible (ASV) 21: Ecclesiastes (version 2) by American Standard Version
description: "Ecclesiastes is a wisdom book of the Old Testament. The author represents himself as the son of David, and king over Israel in Jerusalem. The work consists of personal or autobiographic matter, at times expressed in aphorisms and maxims illuminated in terse paragraphs with reflections on the meaning of life and the best way of life. The work emphatically proclaims all the actions of man to be inherently "vain", "futile", "empty", or "meaningless," depending on translation, as the lives of both wise and foolish men end in death. While the teacher clearly promotes wisdom as a means for a well-lived earthly life, he is unable to ascribe eternal meaning to it. In light of this perceived senselessness, the preacher suggests that one should enjoy the simple pleasures of daily life, such as eating, drinking, and taking enjoyment in one's wife and work, which are gifts from the hand of God." (From Wikipedia, modified by Sam Stinson)

title: Bible (ASV) 20: Proverbs by American Standard Version
description:

(Summary by Sam Stinson)
